title: Global Mobility Business Partner
description:
At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

**This position reports to:**


GBS HR Expert Services Global Mobility M
\_\_

**Mission:**


Serve as strategic business partner and trusted advisor to the Division, developing mobility solutions that align with business strategy. Lead specialized mobility team in delivering exceptional support for complex assignments, building organizational capability through education and training, and creating innovative programs that maximize GM as a business resource and partner.

**Key responsibilities:**

**Strategic Business Partnership:** Act as primary mobility advisor to Division leadership. Develop deep understanding of Division business strategy, talent needs, and organizational challenges. Provide strategic counsel on how mobility can support business objectives and talent strategy. Anticipate future mobility needs and proactively develop solutions.

**Team Leadership \& Case Allocation:** Lead and mentor specialized mobility team in delivering complex assignment support. Allocate cases strategically based on complexity, team expertise, and development opportunities. Provide coaching and guidance on challenging cases, serving as escalation point for complex or sensitive matters. Build team capability through feedback and development.

**Complex Case Advisory:** Serve as sparring partner for Division business leaders on complex, high\-stakes international assignments. Provide specialized guidance on policy interpretation, exception handling, and unique business scenarios. Lead problem\-solving on multi\-country assignments, third\-country nationals, and specialized talent movements.

**Business Education \& Training:** Design and deliver training programs to build Division management and HR capability on mobility strategy, policy, and best practices. Develop educational content for business leaders on talent development through international assignments. Drive awareness of mobility as a strategic tool for succession planning and leadership development.

**Program Creation \& Management:** Develop and manage Division\-specific mobility programs aligned with business strategy (e.g., leadership development programs, talent exchange initiatives, emerging market assignments). Customize policies and processes to address unique Division needs. Launch and oversee special initiatives that enhance mobility effectiveness.

**Compliance \& Risk Management:** Ensure legal and regulatory compliance across all mobility matters. Collaborate with security and legal teams to mitigate risks for assignees and the organization. Maintain health and safety standards for international assignments.

**Organizational Capability Building:** Lead knowledge\-sharing initiatives across the Division. Capture and share mobility best practices and lessons learned. Build a culture of continuous improvement in how mobility supports talent and business objectives.

**Key requirements:**


To be successful in this role, you bring at least 10 years of experience in global mobility, along with a track record of shaping mobility strategies that create value for both the business and its people. You have established yourself as a trusted strategic advisor who leads change with confidence, influencing senior business leaders and partnering effectively across a global organization.


Your expertise spans the full spectrum of international mobility, including immigration, tax, compensation, and policy design. You combine this technical knowledge with strong commercial awareness, enabling you to align mobility solutions with evolving business priorities and the needs of different divisions.


You thrive in complex, international environments where collaboration and influence matter as much as expertise. Whether you are guiding senior stakeholders, navigating organizational change, or solving challenging mobility issues, you bring a strategic mindset, sound judgment, and the ability to build trust at every level.


You are passionate about developing others and have experience designing and delivering engaging training programs that strengthen capabilities across teams. As a leader and mentor, you enjoy helping people grow while fostering a culture of continuous learning and collaboration.


Your analytical thinking allows you to translate data into meaningful insights, while your innovative approach helps you identify opportunities to improve processes and deliver lasting impact. With a strong international perspective and high cultural awareness, you are comfortable working across borders and collaborating with diverse stakeholders around the world.


Finally, you communicate with clarity and confidence in English, both spoken and written. Additional language skills are an advantage and further support your ability to build strong relationships in a global environment.


Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.




Ready to make an impact?


Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Euronext Securities- Compliance Officer
description:
**Job title**

Compliance officer (middle level)


**Department**

Euronext Securities Milan Compliance


**Reporting**

Reporting to the Head of CSDs Compliance and Euronext Securities Milan Chief Compliance Officer


**Tasks and Responsibility**

* Support and proactive collaboration with the local Chief Compliance Officer in the main compliance activities.
* Collaboration in the monitoring relevant legislation, regulations and directives framework (e.g. CSDR, GDPR, AML) applicable to the Company.
* Conduct compliance risk assessments, gap analyses and other compliance analyses to identify and mitigate compliance risks within the central securities depository business.
* Prepare, update and control internal governing documents such as policies and procedures, ensuring alignment with external and group requirements.
* Execute compliance\-related internal controls and thematic reviews
* Preparing regulatory and management reporting, including preparing and presenting compliance reports to senior management and other internal stakeholders.
* Support business units with advice on Euronext Securities activities and maintain continuous dialogue with employees across the organisation regarding compliance\-related topics.
* Coordinate and cooperate with other compliance teams and stakeholders to ensure a harmonised approach.
* Develop presentations and training material and conduct training in accordance with the compliance plan to promote compliance awareness.
* Contribution to the drafting and update of the annual compliance plan and follow up on executed compliance activities.
* Identify and implement activities to develop and mature the compliance culture across the CSDs and business units.
* Contribute to compliance\-related projects as relevant.
* Perform various administration and planning tasks within the compliance area.
* Manage ad hoc tasks as required.

**Qualifications**

* University degree in law, finance, business administration or a related field.
* At least five/six years of experience in compliance, risk management or a related area, preferably within financial services
* Knowledge of relevant legislation, regulations and directives applicable to the financial industry
* Experience in conducting compliance risk assessments, gap analyses and internal controls.
* Proven ability to prepare, update and implement compliance policies, procedures and governing documents.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to advise and support business units and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
* Strong organisational and planning skills, with the ability to proactively manage multiple tasks and projects independently.
* High level of integrity, attention to detail and commitment to maintaining a strong compliance culture.
* Proficiency in English (spoken and written); additional languages are an advantage.

W e are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, gender, age, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, or any other legally protected factor. We value the unique talents of all our people, who come from diverse backgrounds with different personal experiences and points of view and we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect.


**Additional Information**
--------------------------

This job description is only describing the main activities within a certain role and is not exhaustive. It does not prevent to add more tasks, projects.

title: Cards Product Expert
description:
* REQ\-10118123
* 16/07/2026
* Customer Journey
* Milan, Italy
* ING Bank


  
ING Bank N.V. is looking for a Cards and Mobile Payments Expert, acting as Customer Journey Expert, to join one of the Cards \& Mobile Payments squads. The Customer Journey Expert is responsible for the end\-to\-end management, development and optimization of card products and payment journeys, ensuring an outstanding customer experience while balancing business objectives, regulatory requirements and operational efficiency.


Working within a multidisciplinary agile squad, the Cards \& Mobile Payments Expert collaborates closely with Product Owners, IT engineers, Operations, Risk, Compliance, Legal and external partners to define, develop and enhance cards and payments products and services. The role contributes to the evolution of the Cards \& Mobile Payments proposition by identifying customer needs, driving innovation, improving customer journeys and supporting the delivery of strategic initiatives across the full product lifecycle


**Activities****:**


* Manage and optimize debit card, credit card, prepaid card and mobile wallet customer journeys.
* Gather, analyze and document business, customer and regulatory requirements.
* Prepare business cases, market analyses and functional requirements documentation.
* Coordinate product developments and enhancements with internal and external stakeholders.
* Support product launches, feature releases and implementation activities.
* Monitor KPIs, customer behavior and operational performance through reporting and data analysis.
* Conduct process reviews and identify opportunities to improve customer experience and operational efficiency.
* Analyze market trends, customer insights and competitors' offerings to support product evolution.
* Prepare management reports, presentations and performance analyses for governance forums and decision\-making bodies.

**Responsibilities****:**


* Own the end\-to\-end lifecycle of Cards \& Mobile Payments products and customer journeys, ensuring alignment with business objectives and customer needs.
* Drive the continuous improvement of customer experience, digital adoption and operational efficiency across cards and payment journeys.
* Ensure business requirements are clearly defined, prioritized and successfully translated into deliverable solutions.
* Be accountable for the successful delivery of product enhancements, regulatory changes and strategic initiatives within the Cards \& Mobile Payments domain.
* Ensure compliance with internal policies, regulatory requirements and payment scheme obligations.
* Manage and align stakeholders across Product, IT, Operations, Risk, Compliance, Legal, Marketing and external partners.
* Monitor product performance and identify opportunities to improve profitability, customer engagement and business results.
* Contribute to the evolution of the Cards \& Mobile Payments strategy by identifying innovation opportunities and market developments.
* Facilitate collaboration across multidisciplinary teams, helping to remove impediments and drive delivery outcomes.

**Required skills****:**


Experience of at least 2 years in the role


Ability to manage critical situations through good decision\-making skills


Degree in Economics, Management Engineering and similar


Knowledge of the English language


Knowledge of innovative payments with focus on Mobile


**Contract offer:**


**Working conditions**: Permanent employment contract


**Working hours:** Full time (37 hours per week)


**Applicable Collective Labour Agreement:** National Collective Labour Agreement for employees in the credit sector (NCLA)


**Location**: Milan (hybrid)


**Starting annual base salary:** 39\.000€


**Variable remuneration**: in line with the applicable remuneration policies (with a target based on the role, outcomes linked to the Group, Business line/function and individual performance)


Our compensation approach is built on objective, gender\-neutral criteria aligned with the role’s core requirements. That said, we believe talent goes beyond any framework: your total reward package will be tailored to reflect your unique experience, skills, and potential, ensuring we fully recognize the value you bring.


**The benefits of joining ING**


In addition to being a part of a great team, working in a fun and innovative environment, we offer:


* Flexible Smart Working
* Competitive base salaries and performance\-based bonuses
* Meal vouchers and corporate restaurant and bar
* Health insurance and Accident insurance
* Pension fund and welfare plan
* Diverse cultures \& Innovative mindsets
* International Environnent and Talent Program
* Commitment to sustainability
* Lots of training development opportunities to help you grow
* Lots of moments dedicated to physical and mental well\-being
* A special day off when it is your birthday: we call it \#doyourbirthday!
* And of course, we can’t forget free water \& coffee at the office!

**About ING**


ING offers many opportunities to build a diverse and rewarding career. You will be joining an international innovative digital bank, the first in Italy to adopt a fully flexible smart working model, and you will be working in a stimulating environment where you can grow both as an individual and as a professional. Our purpose \- empowering people to stay a step ahead in life and in business \- represents our belief in people’s potential. We don’t judge, coach or to tell people how to live their lives. We empower people and businesses to realize their own vision for a better future.
  

\#doyourthing is our brand direction with us each and every day. It is how we articulate our purpose and our promise to make banking frictionless to the world.
  

‘do your thing’ is about people being free to live the life they want to live, knowing that they will make their world a little better for it.
  

Do you think you are "a step ahead"? !  

  



**Our Commitment**


Diversity is a fundamental element of our corporate culture, and we are fully committed to creating a safe and inclusive environment, based on mutual respect and the value of diversity, offering equal job opportunities to all qualified candidates.


**Job Application Safety Reminder** ️


We’re seeing an increase in fraudulent job offers. To protect yourself, please follow these key guidelines when applying for roles at ING:


* Apply only via official ING platforms: ING uses **Workday** as its internal recruitment system. Applications should be submitted only via our official career site.


* Check the sender’s email carefully: legitimate communication will always come from: @ing.com and/or @myworkday.com


* No payments or banking details will ever be requested. If someone asks for this information, it’s a scam.


If you suspect suspicious activity, report it immediately. Your safety matters to us.

title: Compliance Governance & Controls - Stage extra curriculare
description:
**Data:** 15 lug 2026
  
  

**Località:**  

Milano, Torino, IT
  
  

**\#job\-location.job\-location\-inline { display:** inline; }
  
**Società:** Intesa Sanpaolo S.p.A.
  
  

.
  
  

Overview
  
  

All’interno di Compliance Governance \& Controls, avrai un ruolo integrato tra presidio delle evoluzioni regolamentari e sviluppo delle metodologie di risk assessment.
  
Avrai una visione trasversale e metodologica della Compliance, con coinvolgimento diretto nelle attività di monitoraggio normativo, valutazione del rischio e produzione di strumenti di analisi e reporting a supporto del management.
  
  

Quali saranno le tue attività
  
  

Durante lo svolgimento dello stage, avrai l’opportunità di affiancare e supportare il team della struttura nelle seguenti attività:
  
  

Supporto all’analisi e al monitoraggio delle normative e iniziative regolamentari nazionali ed europee.
  
Classificazione delle normative per ambito, rilevanza e impatto, con contributo a regulatory heatmap e outlook.
  
Supporto alla gestione della Control Room normativa, incluse raccolta, tracking e prioritizzazione delle iniziative.
  
Contributo alle attività di Compliance Risk Assessment, raccolta delle valutazioni di rischio e applicazione delle metodologie di scoring.
  
Collaborazione alla manutenzione ed evoluzione del framework metodologico, metriche e criteri di valutazione.
  
Aggiornamento di risk map, dashboard e viste aggregate del rischio.
  
Predisposizione di report e materiali di sintesi per management e comitati.
  
Supporto all’integrazione del rischio di compliance nei framework aziendali e interazione con le funzioni aziendali.
  
Chi stiamo cercando
  
  

**Se hai le seguenti caratteristiche, ti stiamo aspettando:**  

**Laurea in:**  

* Giurisprudenza (indirizzo diritto bancario/finanziario)
* Economia e Finanza / Banking \& Finance
* Economia dei Mercati e degli Intermediari Finanziari
* Statistica / Data Science / Matematica
* Ingegneria Gestionale


Interesse per tematiche di regolamentazione, compliance e risk management;
  
  

**Conoscenza di base (anche accademica) di:**  

* framework regolamentari europei
* processi di risk assessment
* logiche di scoring e metriche di rischio
* processi di controllo interno


Ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Office (in particolare Excel\-analisi dati e PowerPoint);
  
  

Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.
  
  

Chi siamo
  
  

Siamo leader in Italia e uno dei principali gruppi bancari in Europa. Unisciti a noi e fai parte della nostra storia di successo! Con oltre 20 milioni di clienti in Italia e all'estero siamo un vero e proprio motore di crescita sostenibile con un forte impegno per l'ambiente e un impatto tangibile sulla società.
  
  

Le persone sono al centro, ce ne prendiamo cura impegnandoci nel creare una cultura inclusiva all'interno del Gruppo in cui tutti si sentano protagonisti e valorizzati.
  
  

Unisciti alla nostra realtà internazionale. Il futuro non si aspetta, si sceglie!
  
  

\#sharingfuture
  
  

Garantiamo un ambiente inclusivo e di pari opportunità. Considereremo tutte le candidature a prescindere da razza, religione, orientamento sessuale, identità di genere, stato civile, età, disabilità o qualsiasi altra categoria protetta nel rispetto dei D.lgs. 198/2006, 215/03 e 216/03\.
  
  

Per la valutazione delle candidature, i dati saranno utilizzati da Intesa Sanpaolo S.p.A. come Data Controller. Ti invitiamo a prendere visione dell’Informativa Privacy dedicata.

At Bryden Wood, we are expanding our team in Milan to work on large scale projects for the biggest names in the Data Centre and Pharmaceutical sectors.
  
  

We are looking for an Associate Architect with site experience, to lead the technical delivery of our Data Centre and Pharmaceutical projects. This is your chance to work in a truly international environment, fully integrated with our UK HQ, while enjoying benefits that set a new standard for the Italian market.
  
  

The role
  
  

You will be one of the Architectural leads of our projects in Italy as well as key member on other international projects, working closely with all the other disciplines (Structures, Civils, MEP and Sustainability) and clients to deliver innovative designs.
  
  

* Lead the Architectural design for all project stages (from Concept to Construction) including carrying out regulatory compliance check, concept development, Project coordination, H\&S, Risk management and project delivery.

* Ensure full compliance with Italian regulations.
* Produce, coordinate and possibly lead official submissions to local authorities.
* Manage project coordination, QA/QC procedures, and risk management.
* Mentor junior Architects and liaise with senior management on project escalations.

* Degree in Architecture or equivalent.
* Must\-Have: Deep knowledge of Italian regulations and site experience during construction stages, liaising with General Contractors and some experience with Direzione dei Lavori
* Minimum of 8 years of professional experience, preferable with previous experience in Data Centre or Pharmaceutical projects in Italy.
* Experience in producing, delivering and possibly signing off official submissions to local authorities.
* Language: Full professional fluency in spoken and written Italian and English.
* Project management: Proven experience in project management would be beneficial.
* Revit \& Navisworks: Competent in the use of Revit and Navisworks


Why join us?

* Full\-time employment with CCNL Commercio. The salary will be Competitive and based on experience.
* 9\-Day Fortnight: Enjoy one Friday off every two weeks \- no extra hours required, and no change to your salary.
* Hybrid working: Hybrid working arrangement with a 2 days working from home policy.
* Global Impact: Work on Data Centres and Large\-Scale Pharmaceutical facilities across Europe.
* Paid Professional Membership: We’ve got your membership covered.
* Career Progression: Regular performance reviews to support your growth.
* Learning Opportunities: Regular CPDs to keep you sharp.
* Fun \& Social Events: Team lunches, office breakfasts and snacks, outings and company socials to keep things lively.

description:

Risk Data Aggregation \& Risk Reporting Associate

**Your Perspectives:**


Il team Risk Capital \& Reporting affianca i clienti finanziari nella gestione integrata dei rischi e del capitale, combinando competenze quantitative, regolamentari e di reporting, con focus su capital management, framework di Enterprise Risk, analisi degli impatti normativi sul business e sviluppo di soluzioni per il monitoraggio e la misurazione dei rischi. In particolare, all’interno del nostro team prenderai parte a progetti di disegno e realizzazione di framework di misurazione, gestione e reporting dei rischi per Gruppi Bancari, Assicurativi ed Enti attivi nell’Asset \& Wealth Management, lavorando a stretto contatto con professionisti esperti sul cliente, sia on\-site sia da remoto. Entra nel nostro team e avrai l’opportunità di:

* Supportare attività di analisi normativa in ambito regolamentare (es. framework Basilea), contribuendo a progetti di Regulatory Reporting e Regulatory Strategy.
* Partecipare a progetti di trasformazione in ambito Risk, supportando i clienti nella gestione di iniziative complesse di Change Management.

 **Your****unique****strengths**

 **Set****your****expectation****higher**

title: Global Cybersecurity Operations Manager
description:
**Job Description****Who we are**


Amplifon is an Italian multinational company and the global leader in hearing care solutions and services. More than 20,000 professionals every day, in a network of 9,500 points of sale across 24 countries, give back the joy of hearing, feeling and living to thousands of people across the world.


We believe people are the most important component of our success, and that it is only through strong investment in talent, continuous development, support and recognition that our people can exceed every limit and build a fulfilling career. Aligned to our Winning Workplace, we offer a hybrid working policy allowing employees to work 9 days/month remotely.

**Role Purpose**


Amplifon is looking for a **Global Security Operations Manager** to lead the day\-to\-day execution of the Group's Security Operations, ensuring effective monitoring, detection, response and continuous improvement of cybersecurity capabilities across a multinational environment.


Reporting to the **Group Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)**, the Global Security Operations Manager is responsible for coordinating internal cybersecurity specialists and managed security partners (SOC/MDR, NDR, endpoint protection, incident response providers and specialist vendors), ensuring efficient and resilient security operations on a global scale.


The role combines operational leadership with strong technical expertise, driving continuous improvement across security monitoring, incident response, vulnerability management and security platform operations while operating within the Group's governance and compliance framework.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Security Operations**

  

* Manage the daily execution of the Group Security Operations across endpoint, network, identity and cloud environments.
* Coordinate the Security Operations Center (SOC) and Managed Detection \& Response (MDR) services, ensuring service quality and adherence to agreed SLAs.
* Improve detection capabilities by developing and maintaining SIEM use cases, playbooks and security automation initiatives.
* Monitor operational performance and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.

  

**Incident Response \& Cyber Resilience**  

* Coordinate the end\-to\-end incident response process, including preparation, detection, containment, eradication, recovery and post\-incident reviews.
* Manage major security incidents in collaboration with internal stakeholders and external incident response partners.
* Drive remediation activities and support initiatives aimed at strengthening cyber resilience and operational readiness.

  

**Vulnerability \& Threat Management**  

* Manage the vulnerability management lifecycle, including identification, prioritization, remediation tracking and validation.
* Support threat intelligence activities and exposure management initiatives to prioritize security risks.
* Coordinate penetration testing and security assessment activities with infrastructure and application teams.

  

**Security Engineering \& Platform Operations**  

* Oversee the operational management and continuous improvement of the Group's security technologies, including SIEM, SOAR, EDR/XDR, NDR, email security, firewalls, MFA and privileged access management.
* Collaborate with infrastructure and cloud teams to implement secure configurations and security\-by\-design principles.

  

**Vendor \& Team Coordination**  

* Coordinate a team of security specialists, supporting their development and ensuring effective collaboration across countries.
* Manage relationships with managed security service providers and specialist vendors, monitoring service performance, escalations and operational deliverables.

  

**Reporting \& Planning**  

* Monitor operational KPIs, SLAs and security metrics, providing regular reporting to the Group CISO.
* Support budget planning, capacity planning and continuous improvement initiatives within the Security Operations function.

  

**Governance, Risk \& Compliance**  

* Ensure Security Operations activities comply with the Group Information Security Management System (ISMS), ISO/IEC 27001, NIS2 and applicable regulations.
* Support internal and external audits and contribute to maintaining compliance with security policies and standards.

  

**Candidate Profile****Experience**

  

* 6\-8 years of experience in cybersecurity, including significant experience in Security Operations, SOC or Incident Response environments.
* Previous experience managing security operations teams or coordinating technical cybersecurity professionals.
* Experience working with Managed Security Service Providers (MSSPs) and external security vendors.
* Experience handling cybersecurity incidents and coordinating response activities.
* Exposure to multinational and complex enterprise environments is preferred.

  

**Technical Skills**  

* Strong knowledge of Security Operations technologies, including SIEM/SOAR, EDR/XDR, vulnerability management, threat intelligence and incident response.
* Good understanding of enterprise infrastructure, networking, cloud platforms (Azure, AWS and/or GCP) and identity security.
* Familiarity with security frameworks and standards including ISO/IEC 27001, NIS2, GDPR and NIST.

  

**Leadership \& Soft Skills**  

* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
* Ability to prioritize activities and manage multiple initiatives in a fast\-paced environment.
* Analytical mindset with a continuous improvement approach.
* Collaborative leadership style and ability to work across international teams.

  

**Education \& Certifications**  

* Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Cybersecurity or equivalent experience.
* Professional certifications such as CISSP, CISM, GCIH or similar are considered a plus.
* Fluency in English is mandatory; Italian is a plus.

**What We Offer**

  

* The opportunity to contribute to the security operations of a leading global company operating across 24 countries.
* Close collaboration with the Group CISO and international technology teams.

**Location**: HQ Milan, Italy (Hybrid \- allowing employees to work 6 days per month remotely)


**Compensation \& Benefits**


**Applicable Collective Agreement**: CCNL Commercio (Terziario, Distribuzione e Servizi)


**Contractual Level**:Quadro

**Contract Type**: Permanent \- Full Time


The reference **Gross Annual Salary** is approximately in the range of EUR 70\.000 \- 92\.000 . The actual compensation will be commensurate with the candidate's experience and specific skills and knowledge and also to the educational background.


The position is also eligible for an annual incentive scheme based on Company and individual performance KPIs, with a payout whose target is equal to 15% of the annual gross salary, in line with Company policies.


**Benefits**  

  



* **€1,700 Annual Welfare plan** for your personal well\-being on a wide range of services
* **Health Insurance: QUAS** healthcare coverage and an additional company integrative insurance \+ Accident Insurance
* **Supplementary pension scheme:**option to enrol in the Fondo Fon.Te with employer contributions
* **Office Perks:**On\-site **canteen**and free **company parking**
* **BeWellProgram:**Free access to learning platforms and training programs. We invest in your growth through ongoing learning opportunities, while also offering special corporate discounts, dedicated services for you and your loved ones, and initiatives focused on your wellbeing and physical health

**Equal Opportunities Statement**


Amplifon is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing a diverse and equitable workforce environment. We believe that through valuing our uniqueness and respecting our differences, we can achieve more, and that diversity adds to our culture. We encourage applications from all genders, corners of the world and individual backgrounds.
